trails.name: PATUTU ROUTE
trails.description: Climb to the summit of Patutu (1162 m) for great views of the coast and the North Island.

trails.name: Okiwi Bay Seaward Valley Track
trails.description: Walk or mountain bike through varied vegetation and enjoy ocean views.

trails.name: HINAU TRACK
trails.description: This family-friendly loop track passes through an unusual forest with a number of hīnau trees.

trails.name: MT FYFFE FOREST WALK
trails.description: Listen to forest birds on this short walk through ancient forest.

trails.name: MT FYFFE HUT/KOWHAI SADDLE ROUTE
trails.description: There are superb views from Mt Fyffe. You can climb or mountain bike the steep track to the summit or go as far as Mt Fyffe Hut.

trails.name: POINT KEAN LOOKOUT TO LIMESTONE BAY
PUDDING HILL TRACK
trails.description: A 2 hour walk to the summit of Pudding Hill.

trails.name: Kaikōura Peninsula Walkway
trails.description: Observe seals and seabirds, explore historic pā and whaling sites and enjoy clifftop views of the sea and mountains. Suitable for families, the whole walkway takes three hours but you can also explore a range of shorter tracks.

trails.name: Manuka Bay Track (Port Robinson Walkway)
trails.description: This track hugs the coastline and provides sea views, lush forest and a number of landscape features.

trails.name: Molesworth Recreation Reserve tramping tracks
trails.description: There are five tramping tracks accessible from the Acheron Road; from short tracks from the cob buildings at Molesworth and Acheron to longer tracks with panorama views.

trails.name: Mt Fyffe and the Seaward Kaikōura Range
trails.description: Summer-bare or plastered with winter’s snowy coat, Mount Fyffe and the Seaward Kaikōura Range dominate the Kaikoura skyline. They provide recreation opportunities ranging from a family stroll to a serious backcountry journey.

trails.name: Tweedies Gully Walk
trails.description: This short track takes you though a rare patch of coastal bush rich in bird and plant life to a high point. There are good coastal and sea views. It's an ideal walk for children.

trails.name: Okiwi Bay Lookout Walk
trails.description: Enjoy views of the Kaikōura coastline.

trails.name: Hurunui Walkway (Manuka Bay Track)
trails.description:
From the southern end of Manuka Bay beach, pass through the gate in the fence and follow the track up through the lush broadleaf forest to an open grassy area.

The track crosses this then enters an area of regenerating mānuka and flax. There are examples of tunnel and gulley 'badland' erosion in the clay banks alongside the track.

It then passes through a stretch of mature coastal forest featuring plants such as whauwhaupaku or five-finger, small-leaved kowhai, and a variety of ferns.

The track dips into gullies before rising again to give magnificent views of the coastline and the nearby Hurunui lagoon. There is a headland atop 60 m cliffs where you can rest on a seat and admire the expansive ocean views. 

As the track continues to head south the forest gradually thins out before reaching open farmland. At this point the Hurunui Walkway (external site) crosses private farmland for 10 minutes to reach the Hurunui Mouth Road near the mouth of the Hurunui River.
